description_long: This is a traditional hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a large, rectangular steel blade. The handle appears to be made from a single piece of wood, showing a natural grain and a worn, matte finish, suggesting frequent use. The blade is dark, with visible wear and some surface irregularities, indicating it is forged or cast steel and has seen significant use. The blade is thick and broad, with a single sharp edge and a flat poll on the opposite side, suitable for splitting or chopping wood. The head is mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged fit, with no visible modern fasteners. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, giving it a utilitarian, workmanlike appearance.
description: A traditional hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, worn steel blade, showing signs of frequent use and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the side, clearly showing the full profile of the wooden handle and the broad, rectangular steel blade.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the top, displaying the narrow edge of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite side view reveals the other face of the blade and handle, mirroring the features seen in view 0.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from the rear, showing the flat poll of the blade and the end of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, oval cross-section, and a smooth but worn grip.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular steel blade with a single sharp edge, flat poll, and no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fit with the blade head fitted onto the handle.
